title: Alectorolophus apenninus
intro: Alectorolophus apenninus, also known as Apennine rock rose or Rosmarinus apenninus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Apennine Mountains in Italy. The health benefits of Alectorolophus apenninus include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include diterpenes, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alectorolophus apenninus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, improve digestion, and support respiratory health.
taxonomy: Alectorolophus apenninus is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to Italy and can be found in the Apennine Mountains. The classification of Alectorolophus apenninus includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, and order as Boraginales. This plant is further classified as a genus and species within the family Boraginaceae. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup.
